evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Human voice vs committee 4/10 — Mostly committee-voice boilerplate, with occasional first-person flashes that feel bolted on.

suggestedPatch.target: By focusing on explicit citations, schema structure, and facts authoritative enough for LLMs to quote, organizations can ensure continuous brand relevance even as user search behavior shifts rapidly toward AI-driven answers.
suggestedPatch.before: By focusing on explicit citations, schema structure, and facts authoritative enough for LLMs to quote, organizations can ensure continuous brand relevance even as user search behavior shifts rapidly toward AI-driven answers.
suggestedPatch.after: Cite primary sources, structure definitions with schema, and write quotable sentences—that's most of the game as AI answers replace search clicks.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Mostly committee-voice boilerplate, with occasional first-person flashes that feel bolted on.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — A few sharp takes (E-E-A-T is theater, most AEO advice is garbage) exist but are surrounded by hedged, generic claims.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Two or three memorable lines, but most prose is tri-colon AI cadence ("structuring, clarifying, and validating").

suggestedPatch.target: AEO focuses on structuring, clarifying, and validating content for generative AI models to cite in their responses.
suggestedPatch.before: AEO focuses on structuring, clarifying, and validating content for generative AI models to cite in their responses.
suggestedPatch.after: AEO is the art of writing sentences an LLM can lift without embarrassment.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: Two or three memorable lines, but most prose is tri-colon AI cadence ("structuring, clarifying, and validating").

evidence: Point of view vs both-sides survey 5/10 — Has a POV in flashes but defaults to neutral explainer-comparison mode.

suggestedPatch.target: The top differentiators include schema usage, citation readiness, and focus on helpfulness for direct question-answering.
suggestedPatch.before: The top differentiators include schema usage, citation readiness, and focus on helpfulness for direct question-answering.
suggestedPatch.after: Forget keyword rankings: in AEO you win by being the cleanest quotable source, period.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs both-sides survey" scored 5/10: Has a POV in flashes but defaults to neutral explainer-comparison mode.

evidence: Quotable to a friend 6/10 — "The winners in AEO aren't the loudest—they're the most quotable" and the E-E-A-T line are genuinely quotable; rest isn't.
Intro earns attention in 2 sentences 3/10 — Opens with navigation crumbs, repeated titles, dateline, and a textbook definition—nothing earns attention.

suggestedPatch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the practice of formatting and structuring content to be cited and surfaced by AI answer engines such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the practice of formatting and structuring content to be cited and surfaced by AI answer engines such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: If ChatGPT won't quote you, you don't exist in 2026. AEO is how you become the sentence it steals.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention in 2 sentences" scored 3/10: Opens with navigation crumbs, repeated titles, dateline, and a textbook definition—nothing earns attention.

evidence: intro_hook 6/10 — The TL;DR block is strong, but the actual body intro opens with a dry definition that repeats what the TL;DR already said. The first two sentences of the post body do not earn attention—they restate the topic rather than create tension, surprise, or stakes. The hook needs a concrete inciting incident or data point that makes the reader feel the cost of ignorance before the definition is delivered.

suggestedPatch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the practice of formatting and structuring content to be cited and surfaced by AI answer engines such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the practice of formatting and structuring content to be cited and surfaced by AI answer engines such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: Last month, a B2B SaaS company lost 40% of its top-of-funnel traffic—not to a competitor, but to ChatGPT, which answered their category questions without citing them once.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 6/10: The TL;DR block is strong, but the actual body intro opens with a dry definition that repeats what the TL;DR already said. The first two sentences of the post body do not earn attention—they restate the topic rather than create tension, surprise, or stakes. The hook needs a concrete inciting incident or data point that makes the reader feel the cost of ignorance before the definition is delivered.
